_meta.notes: First town SE of Caelum, on the way to Threadwarden. The market town for human grain — Furrowfolk farmers from across the inland basin bring their single-crop loads here for distribution. The town has three named market days: Wheat Day, Barley Day, Rye Day. The market on each day operates only in that grain. The buyers are mostly elven Salt-folk wholesalers.

tagline: One grain per day. The wholesalers know which day to come.
ambientPool: Sacks of one and only one grain are stacked in a long row across the square.
A Furrowfolk farmer leans on a stake near their load and watches the wholesalers without expression.
An elven Salt-folk in their off-act voice asks blunt questions and gets blunt answers.
The market's bell rings in a different rhythm depending on which grain day it is.

rooms.summary: Open square with the market bell at the center. Stalls along three sides. A weighing-stone in the middle. The fourth side opens onto the river loading dock.
ambientPool: The market bell rings; the day has ended without warning to anyone who didn't already know.
A Furrowfolk family loads their unsold grain back onto a wagon — they will return next month.
A wholesaler counts out coins with one hand while marking a contract with the other.
An apprentice from the Pageborn quarter copies sale records onto orcish paper.

exits.narrative: To the river loading dock where the barges wait.

rooms.summary: Flat barges tied to the dock, ready for the day's purchases. The Furrow runs slow here. Everything moves at barge-pace.
ambientPool: A barge-master ties off a heavy load and steps back to watch it settle.
An elven wholesaler signs a bill of lading without reading it — they have read the same one nineteen times this month.
A river-bird perches on a coil of rope and is left undisturbed.
The afternoon light catches the dust still rising from the morning's threshing.
